description: Build a real-time Network Operations Center (NOC) monitoring platform for a telecom operator's network engineering team. The system must ingest SNMP traps, syslog streams, and NetFlow data from 500+ network devices (routers, switches, base stations), correlate events into incidents using rule-based and ML-assisted engines, display a live topology map with device health status, and escalate P1/P2 outages via PagerDuty and SMS. Use FastAPI and Python 3.11 for the backend event processor, React 18 with WebSockets for the live dashboard, InfluxDB for time-series metrics, and PostgreSQL for incident management. Implement three alert severity levels (P1 critical, P2 major, P3 minor) with auto-escalation rules. Include an SLA reporting module showing MTTR, MTTA, and availability by region and technology domain.
input: Build a real-time NOC monitoring platform for SNMP/syslog ingestion, event correlation, live topology map, and P1/P2 auto-escalation for a telecom network operations team
